findings.title: Recovered bounty proceeds are permanently locked in the contract
All token liquidations are sandwichable because `amountOutMin` is hardcoded to zero

best_agent_match.title: Any single liquidation failure reverts the entire recovery and freezes unrelated assets

best_agent_match.title: Ignored `startPool`/`endPool` results allow false-positive recovery executions

rejected_candidates.title: Any single liquidation failure reverts the entire recovery and freezes unrelated assets
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as duplicative of existing DoS findings. The contract is atomic by design; the reportable risk comes from concrete failure sources already captured in F-005 and F-009 rather than the generic fact that one reverting liquidation rolls back the transaction.

rejected_candidates.title: Ignored `startPool`/`endPool` results allow false-positive recovery executions
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as subsumed by F-007. Ignoring the low-level call results only becomes harmful because the profit gate can be satisfied with preloaded assets; that false-positive execution path is already covered by the existing finding.

rejected_candidates.title: Counter state is fully attacker-controlled
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as non-reportable in this context. `Counter.sol` is a trivial standalone sample contract with intentionally public mutators, and no protocol asset, privilege, or integration-dependent harm is evidenced in the repository.
